channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Declaração de Lula sobre senadores complica relação pré-sabatina de Messias
item.description: President Lula's comment that senators 'think they are God' has heightened tensions with Congress, particularly Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, ahead of Jorge Messias' STF confirmation hearing. Despite the strained atmosphere, government allies claim Messias already has sufficient votes for approval in the CCJ and plenary. The remark was seen by some as a provocation that could undermine political negotiation.

item.title: 'Apodreça na cadeia': Tarcísio sobre tenente-coronel réu por feminicídio
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) commented on the retirement of Lieutenant Colonel Geraldo Leite Rosa Neto, who is detained and charged with the femicide of police officer Gisele and procedural fraud, stating that the accused should 'rot in jail'.

item.title: “Dizer a verdade faz o dedo de muita gente ‘didireita’ cair”, diz Carluxo
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro city councilor Carlos Bolsonaro accused members of the right of trying to 'bury alive' former president Jair Bolsonaro and allowing a narrative that harms the presidential candidacy of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro. He said that simply telling the truth in one or two lines makes the finger of many people on the 'far right' fall. The remarks were made in an interview with O Antagonista.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro pede ao STF autorização para incluir cuidador na prisão domiciliar
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro requested that Minister Alexandre de Moraes of the STF authorize Carlos Eduardo Antunes Torres, brother-in-law of former first lady Michelle Bolsonaro, to act as a caregiver during Bolsonaro's house arrest. The request aims to ensure personal assistance for the former president while he serves a precautionary measure at home.

item.title: Ao atacar o Pix, governo Trump dá presente eleitoral a Lula
item.description: The Trump administration criticized Brazil's Pix system as a threat to U.S. companies, allowing Lula to effortlessly position himself as a defender of national sovereignty. This stance strengthens Lula's image as a nationalist leader resisting external pressures. The attack on Pix has become an unintended electoral gift for the Workers' Party candidate.

item.title: Erika Hilton critica aposentadoria de PM suspeito de matar mulher em SP
item.description: Federal deputy Erika Hilton (Psol-SP) criticized the pension granted to Lieutenant Colonel Geraldo Leite Rosa Neto, suspected of killing his wife, and the lenient treatment by São Paulo's Military Police and Military Justice, blaming Governor Tarcísio de Freitas for favoring police accused of crimes.

item.title: Lula sanciona reajuste de 8% para servidores do MPU em 2026, mas veta parcelas de 2027 e 2028
item.description: President Lula sanctioned an 8% salary increase for MPU and CNMP employees effective July 2026, but vetoed the 2027 and 2028 installments, citing the Fiscal Responsibility Law and the risk of creating mandatory spending beyond his term. Unions called it a partial victory, arguing the veto undermines the three-year inflation loss recovery plan, and the SindMPU is mobilizing to overturn the vetoes in Congress.

item.title: Marinho admite divergências com Caiado e prevê aliança no segundo turno
item.description: Senator Rogério Marinho, opposition leader in the Senate and coordinator of Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-campaign, stated that Ronaldo Caiado's presidential pre-candidacy will deepen debate within the right-wing field but predicted unity in the second round against President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. He acknowledged differences among right-wing candidates in the first round but assured they would unite with the common goal of defeating the current government.

item.title: “Se é do PL e não menciona apoio a Flávio, não merece confiança”, diz Carluxo
item.description: Carlos Bolsonaro (PL), known as Carluxo, stated in an X post that Liberal Party candidates who do not declare support for his brother, Flávio Bolsonaro (RJ), do not deserve trust. He urged his supporters to reject such candidates in the 2026 elections. The statement reinforces familial coordination within the PL to consolidate backing for Flávio as a prospective presidential candidate.

item.title: ANP fiscaliza leilões de gás de cozinha da Petrobras por suspeita de preços elevados
item.description: The ANP launched an inspection into Petrobras' cooking gas (GLP) auctions following suspicions of prices above Import Parity Prices (PPI), prompted by President Lula's statements. The auction manager was removed after conducting the auction against corporate direction, and the investigation covers all of Petrobras' production hubs. The action is grounded in MP 1.340/2026, which expanded ANP's authority to investigate abusive price increases and unjustified supply refusals.

item.title: Deputada federal Helena da Asatur troca MDB pelo PSD e anuncia pré-candidatura ao Senado
item.description: Federal Deputy Helena da Asatur of the PSD announced her pre-candidacy for the Senate from Roraima in the 2026 elections after leaving the MDB. She expressed support for Ronaldo Caiado’s presidential pre-candidacy. Although she is the only woman representing Roraima in the Chamber of Deputies and has a background as a transportation businesswoman, she faces eight years of ineligibility due to a first-instance conviction for vote-buying in the 2024 municipal elections, a ruling her defense plans to appeal.

item.title: Marqueteiro Sidônio manda Lula falar: “O Pix é nosso”
item.description: President Lula defended the Pix payment system as a national achievement during an event in Salvador, following a prompt from Communications Minister Sidônio Palmeira. The statement came a day after a U.S. trade report criticized Pix for creating potential distortions in international commerce, underscoring the government's narrative of technological sovereignty.

item.title: Ciro rebate Lula: “Esse defeito da roubalheira não aconteceu”
item.description: Ciro Gomes (PSDB) rebutted President Lula's (PT) characterization of him as 'hot-tempered', acknowledging personal flaws but firmly denying any involvement in corruption, stating 'this defect of corruption did not happen'. He argued his political trajectory was not harmed by illegal acts.

item.title: Rio de Janeiro e Rondônia não vão reduzir ICMS sobre combustível
item.description: Rio de Janeiro and Rondônia will not join the federal proposal to subsidize ICMS on imported diesel, according to Vice President Geraldo Alckmin. The temporary measure aims to curb fuel price increases caused by the Middle East war, offering a R$ 1.20 per liter subsidy for two months, split equally between the federal government and states. Although some governors initially resisted, 90% of states have already agreed to the plan.

item.title: 10 pontos para dialogar com o conservadorismo sem ceder princípios
item.description: The article argues that economic policies alone are insufficient to gain popular support in Brazil, as the moral dimension of politics is equally, if not more, important. It highlights how Bolsonarism consolidated a moral conservatism rooted in individualist, familial, Christian, machista, racist, and colonial values, and that progressives must engage in dialogue with conservatives without compromising principles of equality and autonomy, using agendas that can solidify support and weaken conservative resistance to social liberation.

item.title: Advogado de Heleno que pediu para jantar durante julgamento lança candidatura para deputado federal
item.description: Lawyer Matheus Mayer Milanez, who defended General Augusto Heleno in the STF during the September 2025 coup plot trial, announced his pre-candidacy for federal deputy from the Federal District. He gained notoriety for requesting hearing delays to have dinner and using phrases like 'argumentative flat-earthism' and 'toró de parpite' during the proceedings.

item.title: 'Não esqueça de falar do PIX', orienta Sidônio a Lula antes de presidente citar relatório do governo Trump
item.description: During an event in Bahia, the Minister of the Presidency’s Secretariat of Communication, Sidônio Palmeira, urged President Lula to mention the PIX in his speech. Lula then referenced a U.S. government report criticizing PIX for distorting international trade and declared that no one will make Brazil change the system, emphasizing its national value and potential for improvement.

item.title: Moreira Franco lança livro sobre bastidores da política brasileira
item.description: Wellington Moreira Franco will launch his book "Politics as Destiny – Paths and Missteps of Redemocratization" on April 8, 2026, at Casa ParlaMento in Brasília. The 1,000-page work, based on over 100 hours of interviews with sociologist Aspásia Camargo and philosopher Denis Rosenfield, includes testimonies from 18 figures and traces his political career as Rio de Janeiro governor and federal minister, with attendance expected from MDB leaders and TCU President Vital do Rêgo.

item.title: CPI do Crime remarca depoimento de Cláudio Castro para 14 de abril
item.description: The Senate's Organized Crime CPI has rescheduled the testimony of former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) for April 14, after he requested a postponement of the originally scheduled hearing. Castro confirmed he will attend and plans to present data on public security during his tenure. He was recently declared ineligible by the TSE for abuse of political and economic power, following his resignation just one day before the ruling.

item.title: Em Salvador, Lula exibe força política e intensifica agenda para conter desgaste no Nordeste
item.description: President Lula visited Salvador to announce urban mobility investments, strengthening his political presence in the Northeast amid rising rejection and the threat posed by ACM Neto in Bahia. He urged militias to combat digital disinformation and praised Rui Costa, emphasizing the importance of truth in the 2026 elections.

item.title: Senador aciona Justiça contra governo por disparo de mensagens sobre IR
item.description: Senator Rogério Marinho (PL-RN) filed a lawsuit in the Federal District Court against the Lula government for sending mass WhatsApp messages via the Gov.br platform to announce income tax exemption for those earning up to R$ 5,000 monthly. He challenges the use of personal data, claims the message has a promotional and electoral character, and requests details on the databases used and number of recipients. The Ministry of Finance defends the message as informative, while the Federal Revenue Agency denies conducting WhatsApp communications.

item.title: Flávio tem 52% e Lula tem 33,5% na disputa pelo Planalto no Paraná
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) leads the presidential race in the state of Paraná with 52% of voting intentions in the first round, according to an AtlasIntel poll released on April 2, 2026. President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) follows with 33.5%, within the poll's 3-point margin of error. The survey, registered with the TSE under BR-05315/2026, interviewed 1,254 voters between March 25 and 30 and cost R$75,000, funded entirely by the polling firm.

item.title: Presidente de central diz que recuo na 'taxa das blusinhas' teria impacto negativo sobre emprego
item.description: Ricardo Patah, president of the UGT (General Union of Workers), criticized the Lula government's potential rollback of the 'blusinhas tax' — a 20% levy on imported goods under $50, especially from Asian e-commerce platforms — arguing it would negatively affect employment in Brazil. He defended maintaining the tax as a way to protect national industry and workers from unfair competition with low-cost Asian products.

item.title: “Que apodreça na cadeia”, diz Tarcísio sobre coronel que matou esposa
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas stated that Lieutenant Colonel Geraldo Leite Rosa Neto, accused of killing his wife, continues to receive his military pension per regulations, but he expects a severe punishment, including loss of rank and position. He said that after expulsion from the force, benefits would go to the victim's family, and that he hopes the accused 'rots in jail' for the rest of his life.

item.title: Advogado de Heleno que pediu tempo para jantar durante julgamento anuncia pré-candidatura à Câmara
item.description: Matheus Mayer Milanez, the lawyer who became famous for asking to delay a session to have dinner during the trial of General Augusto Heleno in the STF, announced his pre-candidacy for the Chamber of Deputies under the Republicans party of the Federal District. He gained notoriety after joking with Justice Alexandre de Moraes about meal times during the interrogations of the coup plot's 'core nucleus'. Milanez is a UnB graduate, specialist in Criminal Law and Criminal Procedure, and currently pursuing a master's degree in Criminal Law at the same university.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro pede ao STF para que irmão de Michelle seja cuidador
item.description: The defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro has requested that Minister Alexandre de Moraes of the Supreme Federal Court appoint Carlos Eduardo Antunes Torres, the foster brother of former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro, as a caregiver during Bolsonaro's house arrest. The petition argues that Michelle, her daughter, and stepdaughter rely on family support for their well-being during the period of restriction.

item.title: ‘Não existe almoço grátis’ economistas comentam decisão de Lula
item.description: Economists warn that Lula’s government decision on fuel prices, influenced by political factors beyond international markets, risks creating distortions, product shortages, and inflationary pressure—especially in diesel and aviation kerosene—with ripple effects on food, transport, and consumption. Marianna Costa of Mirae Asset Brasil stresses that state intervention has limits amid external oil pressure, while Frederico Zornig of Quantiz Pricing Solutions criticizes the politicization of Petrobras’ pricing policy, which misaligns with market dynamics and raises supply risks.

item.title: Governo deve chamar mais de 7 mil aprovados em concursos neste ano
item.description: The Minister of Management and Innovation in Public Services, Esther Dweck, announced that the federal government will summon over 7,000 approved candidates from public competitions in 2025, including 3,600 from the National Unified Public Competition (CPNU) and 1,500 from the Federal Police, with appointments to begin in early May. She also noted that sectors such as health, mining, and regulation will be reviewed for future hiring.

item.title: Toffoli voou em aviăo de empresa de Vorcaro para ir ao Tayayá, indicam documentos da Anac
item.description: Documents from Anac and Decea indicate that STF Minister Dias Toffoli flew on July 4, 2025, on an aircraft operated by Prime Aviation, a company in which Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, was a shareholder. The flight was destined for Tayayá, raising concerns about potential conflicts of interest and the use of private resources by public officials. Folha de S.P.H. obtained the records, which suggest a connection between the minister and a financial sector businessman.

item.title: Crusoé: Lula posa de defensor do Pix
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) defended the Pix payment system after it was cited in a U.S. government report as a potential trade barrier. His remarks were prompted and completed by Communications Minister Sidônio Palmeira, who reminded Lula to explicitly mention Pix during his statement.

item.title: Governo explora críticas dos EUA ao Pix e amplia confronto entre Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The U.S. government criticized Brazil's Pix payment system in a trade report, claiming it distorts international commerce. The Lula administration used the criticism to reinforce its national sovereignty narrative, accusing Flávio Bolsonaro of seeking foreign interference in Brazilian elections through ties to Donald Trump and participation in CPAC. Lula and other officials defended Pix as a national success and refused to change the model.

item.title: PT vai usar inserção na TV para atacar Flávio Bolsonaro em estreia de nova estratégia de Lula
item.description: The PT will use its TV and radio ad time to attack Flávio Bolsonaro, marking the debut of a new strategy in Lula's campaign. The offensive follows polls showing Lula stagnating and Bolsonaro rising, including a technical tie in a potential second-round scenario according to Genial Quaest. The strategy also includes social media actions and grassroots work in key cities, linking Bolsonaro to an alleged 'political project based on attacking democracy'.

item.title: PT reage a relatório de Trump sobre Pix e ataca Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The PT reacted to U.S. government criticism of Brazil's Pix payment system, accusing Senator Flávio Bolsonaro of supporting Donald Trump to undermine the system. The party defends Pix as a symbol of national sovereignty, emphasizing it was created by Brazil and should not be changed due to foreign pressure. Lula stated that no one will change Pix, given its service to Brazilian society.

item.title: Tarcísio sanciona lei que autoriza SP a fazer empréstimos de R$ 15 bi
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) signed a law authorizing the São Paulo state government to take out loans of up to US$ 2.8 billion (about R$ 15 billion) to fund infrastructure and social projects. The funds will primarily support the completion of the Rodoanel (North, East, and West sections), the Poverty Overcoming Program, state debt sustainability improvements, and a public-private partnership for the New Administrative Center. The measure aims to fulfill existing contractual obligations, covering the gap between initial cost estimates and approved updated project costs.

item.title: Alckmin diz que dois estados decidiram não aderir à subvenção ao diesel importado
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin announced that only Rio de Janeiro and Rondônia have refused to join the federal subsidy program for imported diesel, while two or three other states are still evaluating their position until Friday. The measure, which proposes a R$1.20 per liter subsidy (R$0.60 from the Union and R$0.60 from states), aims to mitigate the impact of rising oil prices due to Middle East tensions and prevent cost pass-through to consumers, with a temporary duration of up to two months.

item.title: “Lula e nós salvamos a democracia”, diz Alckmin
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin (PSB) stated that he and President Lula (PT) saved democracy by winning the last elections. Confirmed on the reelection ticket, he said the campaign should, at the appropriate time, compare Lula's government with that of former President Jair Bolsonaro (PL).

item.title: Ministro da Previdência desiste de candidatura e segue no governo Lula
item.description: Social Security Minister Wolney Queiroz (PDT) announced he is withdrawing his candidacy for the Chamber of Deputies in 2026 at President Lula’s request, choosing to remain in office until December to focus on combating fraud and reducing INSS backlogs. He assumed the role in May 2025 after Carlos Lupi resigned amid an investigation into illegal pension deductions. Queiroz, a six-term federal deputy who ran unsuccessfully for re-election in 2022, has been a PDT member since 1992.

item.title: Congresso reprova ou deixa caducar 77% das MPs de Lula; taxa de aprovação é a menor desde 2001
item.description: Congress approved only 23% of the provisional measures issued by Lula in his third government, the lowest rate since the EC 32 of 2001, with 77% of MPs expiring without deliberation. This 'silent veto' reflects the declining effectiveness of the executive instrument over the past two decades, regardless of the party in power. The downward trend in approval rates began after the peak of 90.4% in Lula's first term and reached 68.3% under Bolsonaro.

item.title: O que explica o crescimento de Flávio Bolsonaro nas pesquisas recentes, segundo Marinho
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro's rise in the polls is attributed to increased public awareness of his political profile, shifting perception from a regional senator to a conservative, dialoguing, and flexible candidate. According to Rogério Marinho, coordinator of the pre-campaign, voters now see him as a young, negotiable figure who is not inflexible, overcoming initial rejection due to lack of familiarity.

item.title: “Não tem apoio ao Flávio? Descarte-o, ainda que do PL”, diz Eduardo
item.description: Former deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro urged supporters on Thursday (2) not to vote for candidates who do not support his brother Flávio Bolsonaro, even if they are from the PL party. He argued that voting for those who do not support Flávio only helps Lula and advised voters to apply this criterion in elections for senator, federal deputy, or state deputy.

item.title: Lula diz que Petrobras vai anular leilão que elevou preço do gás de cozinha
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva announced that Petrobras will annul the LPG (cooking gas) auction held on Tuesday, which resulted in prices above reference levels and was deemed an act of insubordination against government and company leadership. Lula criticized the auction as 'cretinice and bandidagem,' stating that poor Brazilians will not bear the cost of the Middle East conflict, and emphasized that the government will not allow increases in the 13kg gas cylinder, which could rise by up to R$ 39.40.

item.title: Com transmissão na TV, Universal prepara megaevento em 9 estádios
item.description: The Universal Church of the Kingdom of God is holding the 'Family at the Foot of the Cross' event across nine Brazilian stadiums and arenas this Good Friday, with TV Record broadcast and free admission. Bishop Renato Cardoso, positioned as Edir Macedo’s successor, and his wife Cristiane Cardoso serve as ambassadors, emphasizing spiritual solutions to family crises. While framed as evangelistic, the event has drawn skepticism from former members regarding its actual motives.

item.title: TSE retoma caso Denarium após adiamentos que permitiram renúncia estratégica
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) will resume on Friday, April 10, the trial that could render former Roraima governor Antonio Denarium ineligible, following a series of delays that allowed him to remain in office until his strategic resignation last Friday, April 27. He was convicted in 2022 by the Roraima Regional Electoral Court for the irregular use of social programs ('Cesta da Família' and 'Morar Melhor') to gain electoral advantages, and now faces disqualification from running for Senate in the 2026 elections, similar to what happened to Cláudio Castro in Rio de Janeiro.

item.title: Fernando Morais interrompe entrevista para atender ligação de Lula
item.description: Writer Fernando Morais interrupted a live interview on the Jornal da Bahia no Ar program to take a call from President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who wanted to know about the launch of the second volume of his biography, 'Lula, Volume 2'. Morais returned to the interview and said Lula called to ask how the event in São Paulo went, where he developed tendinitis from signing many autographs.

item.title: Virada no TSE e aposta no desconhecido: o jogo arriscado do PL de Flávio no Rio
item.description: Governor Cláudio Castro is seeking to overturn his ineligibility at the TSE through potential changes in the Court’s composition, aiming to remain viable for a future Senate run. Meanwhile, the Rio PL is backing little-known candidate Douglas Ruas for the state election — initially favoring an indirect vote via the State Legislative Assembly to avoid a direct clash with Eduardo Paes, but preparing a direct candidacy as a long-term strategy to build name recognition, with Flávio Bolsonaro acting as a key behind-the-scenes operator.

item.title: É defesa do emprego e da renda, diz Alckmin sobre taxa das blusinhas
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin defended the 'blusinhas tax' as a measure to protect employment and income in Brazil's apparel sector, stating that domestic producers still pay more taxes than importers even with the levy. He noted that the taxation on international purchases under US$50, in effect since August 2024, aims to ensure fair competition and was inserted into the Mover Law as a 'jabuti'. Alckmin said he does not participate in the debate about the impact of removing this tax on the 2026 elections.

item.title: Wolney Queiroz desiste de candidatura e seguirá na Previdência Social
item.description: Minister of Social Security Wolney Queiroz (PDT) announced he will not leave his position to run in the 2026 elections, following guidance from President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT). He will remain in office to continue key government actions, particularly managing the INSS, reducing backlogs, and overseeing reimbursement programs. The decision aims to preserve stability in sensitive areas by avoiding the departure of technical officials during the electoral process, reflecting Planalto’s concern over criticisms from the INSS CPMI.

item.title: Coordenador de campanha descarta novo ‘Posto Ipiranga’ para Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Rogério Marinho, coordinator of Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-campaign, dismissed the need for a new 'Ipiranga Post' in the economy, stating that fiscal responsibility was already established during Bolsonaro's government and there is no longer uncertainty about the right's economic management. He criticized the Lula government for fiscal irresponsibility, tax increases, and high interest rates, and said Flávio Bolsonaro's proposal aims to revive deregulation, legal security, and private sector partnerships.

item.title: Eliziane deixa o PSD após escolha por Caiado e se filia ao PT
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama (PT-MA) left the PSD and joined the PT on April 2, 2026, after the party selected Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate. She criticized the PSD’s new political direction, stating her thinking differs, and reaffirmed her commitment to social justice in Maranhão. Eliziane was the rapporteur of the January 8 CPMI that recommended the indictment of Jair Bolsonaro and 60 others.

item.title: Aliados veem pavimentação de caminho para Nunes disputar governo de SP em 2030
item.description: Allies of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas and São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes believe the path for the emedebista to run for governor of São Paulo in 2030 is being paved, especially after Vice Governor Felicio Ramuth joined the MDB. This move is seen as part of a 'married game' to strengthen Nunes’ ticket, with explicit support from Tarcísio, who had previously signaled preference for Nunes as his successor. Ramuth’s exit from the PSD, following disagreements with Gilberto Kassab over the vice-governor slot, underscores the strategic alignment between parties for the state succession.
